one_time_description: Settlement Proceeds of $506,389 recognized as revenue
economic_notes: Band owns Adams Lake Indian Band Development Corporation (100%). Band operates timber licenses with silviculture obligations. Revenue from Forest Consultation and Revenue Sharing Agreements of $3,404,942. Lease revenue from Pierre's Point and Sandy Point operations. Property tax lease accounts maintained.
notes: Audit is qualified for two reasons: (1) PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations not yet assessed as of audit report date - impact unknown; (2) Government Business Enterprise (ALDC) financial statements not prepared under IFRS as required by PSAS - impact unknown. Band consolidated two social housing funds as subsidiaries and accounts for Adams Lake Indian Band Development Corporation under modified equity method as a GBE. New long-term debt of $4,012,428 issued during year including a $2,500,000 RBC term loan (interest only). Adjustment of $1,512,428 on long-term debt proceeds on social housing construction charged to accumulated surplus. Total government transfers calculated as: ISC $9,283,658 + CMHC $419,330 + First Nations Health Authority $1,302,236 + First Nations Education Steering Commission $864,714 + First Peoples Cultural Council $433082 + New Relationship Trust $260,000 + Province of BC $3,062,277 + Secwepemc Child and Family Services $335,869 + Thompson Nicola Regional District $73,723 + BC First Nations Gaming $572,219 + BC Hydro $6,000 = $16,613,108 plus Insurance proceeds $299,640 bringing identifiable government/public transfers to approximately $16,912,748; OSR calculated conservatively. Schedule of remuneration not included in provided financial statement text.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
